text.title: Judge Calibration Protocol Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes when calibrating LLM-as-judge with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Skipped calibration — judge ships on the basis of "looked right on 3 examples we tried".
antipattern.symptom: Production dashboard shows 99% pass while a hand-spot-check finds clear violations slipping through.
antipattern.root-cause: Cognitive bias of the prompt author confirms the prompt is fine; small sample sizes amplify confirmation bias.
antipattern.fix: Run the full protocol against a 30-case holdout before any production use.

antipattern.description: Contaminated holdout — holdout cases used as positive examples in the judge prompt.
antipattern.symptom: κ on holdout = 0.95; real-world κ measured a quarter later = 0.42.
antipattern.root-cause: Author reuses convenient cases; no holdout-vs-prompt audit step.
antipattern.fix: Prompt-hash recorded; CI step checks no holdout-id appears in the prompt; rotate fixture quarterly.

antipattern.description: Single-rater labels treated as ground truth.
antipattern.symptom: κ ≈ 1.0 against own labels but Issue tracker fills with disputes after launch.
antipattern.root-cause: One rater's bias becomes "truth"; achievable κ ceiling unknown.
antipattern.fix: Add a second rater; report inter-rater κ alongside judge κ; treat judge κ ceiling as inter-rater κ.

antipattern.symptom: Judge prompt was tweaked twice, model upgraded once, no new κ value; gate now measures something different from intent.
antipattern.root-cause: "Calibration" was treated as an event, not a process.
antipattern.fix: Trigger calibration on every prompt change, model swap, or quarterly cadence (whichever first); CI enforces freshness.

antipattern.description: Single-failure-class fixture — holdout has 95% "refused" / 5% "complied", judge learns "always say refused" and scores high κ accidentally.
antipattern.symptom: κ ≥ 0.7 but real-world false-pass rate is 50% (judge never catches compliance).
antipattern.root-cause: Class imbalance in holdout; κ underestimates the imbalance penalty.
antipattern.fix: Stratified holdout: at least 30% of the minority class; report per-class precision/recall in addition to κ.
